meanings.en: 深入調查、仔細探查
meanings.zh: to investigate thoroughly
examples: The PM probed deeper into why users were dropping off.
She probed the interviewee with follow-up questions.

meanings.en: 調查；探針（醫療或太空用）
meanings.zh: an investigation; or a device used to explore
examples: The audit was a probe into the team's decision-making process.
NASA launched a new space probe toward Jupiter.

similar.diff: 更正式；probe 強調深入細節
examine 較全面；probe 強調主動追問
scrutinize 更強調一字一句地檢視

lookalikes.zh: 調查 vs 證明
lookalikes.tip: probe = 探查過程；prove = 得出結論，兩個字只差一個字母

lookalikes.zh: 探查 vs 戳、催促
lookalikes.tip: prod = 用手指或棍子戳；也比喻催促某人做事，和 probe 語感相近但不同

meanings.en: 細微的差異或差別（語氣、意義、情境上的）
meanings.zh: a very slight difference in meaning, feeling, or tone
examples: A good PM understands the nuance between user needs and user wants.
The nuance in her tone suggested she wasn't fully convinced.

meanings.en: 細緻地表達或區分（較少用）
meanings.zh: to express or consider with subtle distinctions
examples: He nuanced his argument to avoid overgeneralizing.

similar.diff: 更強調隱藏或不明顯
distinction 較明顯；nuance 更微妙
shade of meaning = 意義的細微層次，與 nuance 幾乎同義

lookalikes.zh: 細微差異 vs 立場
lookalikes.tip: stance = 立場或姿勢，字尾相同但意思不同

lookalikes.zh: 細緻入微的 vs 增強的
lookalikes.tip: enhanced = 被增強、被提升的，字尾相似但意思完全不同

meanings.en: 強烈的吸引力或著迷感
meanings.zh: the state of being intensely attracted or interested
examples: There's a fascination with data that drives our analytics culture.
His fascination with user behavior led to several product breakthroughs.

meanings.en: 令人著迷的事物或特質
meanings.zh: a quality or thing that attracts great interest
examples: The ocean holds a deep fascination for scientists and explorers alike.

similar.diff: obsession 更強烈，常帶負面意味，難以自拔
較少用，fascination 更常見
intrigue 更強調因神秘感而產生的好奇

lookalikes.zh: 著迷 vs 法西斯主義
lookalikes.tip: 字首相同但完全不同：fascism 是政治意識形態

lookalikes.zh: 令人著迷的 vs 感到著迷的
lookalikes.tip: fascinating 描述事物（主動）；fascinated 描述人的感受（被動）——和 interesting/interested 一樣的邏輯

meanings.en: （讓某人）加入或報名（課程、計劃、機構）
meanings.zh: to officially register or join a program, course, or organization
examples: We enrolled 500 beta users in our early access program.
She enrolled in an MBA program last fall.

meanings.en: 說服某人加入或參與
meanings.zh: to persuade someone to join a cause or activity
examples: He managed to enroll three senior engineers in the initiative.

collocations.zh: 自動加入（如退休金計劃）

similar.diff: 更廣泛，任何正式記錄都可用
更非正式
subscribe 更常用於數位服務或內容

lookalikes.zh: 美式 vs 英式拼法
lookalikes.tip: 美式雙 l；英式單 l，但都是同一個字

lookalikes.zh: 美式 vs 英式名詞拼法
lookalikes.tip: 同樣的規則，美式多一個 l

meanings.en: 逐漸消耗、削弱（精力、信心、資源）
meanings.zh: to gradually weaken or drain energy, confidence, or resources
examples: Endless meetings sap the team's energy and creativity.
Micromanagement saps employee morale over time.

meanings.en: 樹液
meanings.zh: the liquid that flows through a plant
examples: The maple tree produces sap in spring, which is made into syrup.

meanings.en: （俚語）容易被騙的傻瓜
meanings.zh: (slang) a foolish person who is easily deceived
examples: Don't be a sap — read the contract before you sign.

family.zh: 傻乎乎的；過度感情用事的

similar.diff: drain 更強調把東西抽走；sap 強調緩慢削弱
deplete 更正式，常用於資源
erode 強調長期慢慢損耗，和 sap 很像但更偏物理或抽象結構

lookalikes.zh: 消耗 vs 快速攻擊／消滅
lookalikes.tip: zap = 快速擊中或消滅，語感更有力

lookalikes.zh: 消耗 vs 小睡
lookalikes.tip: nap = 小睡一下；只差第一個字母，注意別看錯

meanings.en: 對某事同時持有正反兩面感受，無法決定
meanings.zh: having mixed or contradictory feelings about something
examples: I'm ambivalent about launching this feature — the data is promising but the risk is high.
She felt ambivalent about the promotion: excited but also scared.

meanings.en: 態度不明確的、模稜兩可的
meanings.zh: uncertain or unclear in one's position
examples: The committee's ambivalent response made it hard to move forward.

similar.diff: 更口語；ambivalent 更精確指兩種相反情感並存
indecisive 強調無法決定；ambivalent 強調情感的矛盾
torn 更口語，強調被兩個選項拉扯的感覺

lookalikes.zh: 情感矛盾 vs 意思模糊
lookalikes.tip: ambiguous = 含義不清；ambivalent = 情感上左右為難

lookalikes.zh: 矛盾情感 vs 等值、等價
lookalikes.tip: equivalence = 兩者等值的狀態；字尾相同但意思完全不同

meanings.en: 某事物的最典型代表或縮影
meanings.zh: a perfect example or embodiment of a quality or type
examples: She is the epitome of a data-driven PM.
That product is the epitome of good UX design.

meanings.en: （文學）摘要、縮略版（古典用法）
meanings.zh: a summary or abstract of a text
examples: The author wrote an epitome of the longer work.

family.zh: 被體現的；體現了（過去式）

similar.diff: 更強調「把某種特質化為實體」
更正式、文學性強
paragon 強調道德上或品質上的完美典範

lookalikes.zh: 典型 vs 集、插曲
lookalikes.tip: 都是 epi- 開頭，但完全不同

lookalikes.zh: 體現 vs 墓誌銘
lookalikes.tip: epitaph = 刻在墓碑上的悼念文字；都以 epi- 開頭但語義差距極大

meanings.en: 極度混亂、無秩序的
meanings.zh: in a state of complete confusion or disorder
examples: The sprint planning was chaotic — no one agreed on priorities.
Startup environments can feel chaotic but also energizing.

meanings.en: （數學／物理）混沌的（指混沌理論）
meanings.zh: relating to chaos theory — sensitive to initial conditions
examples: The weather system is inherently chaotic and hard to predict.

family.zh: 混沌中立（角色扮演遊戲術語，現常用於形容人）

similar.diff: turbulent 更常形容局勢；chaotic 強調秩序全無
disorganized 較輕；chaotic 程度更強
frantic 強調因焦慮而忙亂；chaotic 強調秩序的崩潰

lookalikes.zh: 混亂的 vs 異國風情的
lookalikes.tip: 字尾相同 -otic，但意思完全不同

lookalikes.zh: 混亂 vs 深淵、鴻溝
lookalikes.tip: chasm = 巨大的裂縫或分歧，字首相近但意思不同

meanings.en: 比喻、隱喻（直接說某事「是」另一件事，不用 like 或 as）
meanings.zh: a figure of speech where one thing is described as another
examples: 'This product is a leaky bucket' is a metaphor for high churn.
Roadmaps are a metaphor for strategic direction, not a promise.

meanings.en: （比喻用法）象徵某事物的概念或意象
meanings.zh: something used as a symbol for something else
examples: The startup is a metaphor for the founder's own journey.

similar.zh: 明喻（用 like 或 as）
similar.diff: simile 說「像」；metaphor 說「是」
analogy 是完整的比較說明；metaphor 是直接替換
allegory 是整篇故事都是比喻；metaphor 只是單一概念替換

lookalikes.zh: 比喻 vs 形而上學
lookalikes.tip: 同樣 meta- 開頭（希臘文「超越」），但完全不同領域

lookalikes.zh: 比喻的 vs 字面的
lookalikes.tip: 這兩個詞是反義詞，經常成對出現：literally vs metaphorically

meanings.en: 荒野、未開發的自然地區
meanings.zh: a large natural area with no human habitation
examples: The team retreated to the wilderness for an offsite.
Alaska is known for its vast wilderness.

meanings.en: （比喻）政治失勢期；迷茫期
meanings.zh: a period of being out of power or out of favor
examples: After the failed launch, the product team spent months in the wilderness.
The politician returned from years in the wilderness.

collocations.zh: 在荒野中；處於失勢狀態
曠野中的聲音（孤掌難鳴）
在荒野中徘徊（比喻：迷失方向）

similar.diff: wild 是形容詞；wilderness 是名詞
特指澳洲地理
hinterland 偏地理用語，指遠離中心的邊遠地區

lookalikes.zh: 荒野 vs 野性、狂亂
lookalikes.tip: wildness 是 wild 的名詞形式；wilderness 特指無人荒野

lookalikes.zh: 荒野 vs 野生動物
lookalikes.tip: wildlife = 野生動物（集合名詞）；wilderness = 荒野地區

meanings.en: （有目的的）探險、遠征行動
meanings.zh: a journey made for a specific purpose, often exploration
examples: The team treated the product discovery as an expedition into unknown user territory.
They organized an expedition to the Arctic.

meanings.en: （正式）迅速、效率
meanings.zh: speed and efficiency
examples: Please handle this matter with all due expedition.

family.zh: 遠征的（如 expeditionary force 遠征軍）

similar.diff: journey 更廣泛；expedition 強調有目的、有組織的探索
mission 更強調目標導向
quest 帶有更強的文學或英雄主義色彩

lookalikes.zh: 探險 vs 加速處理
lookalikes.tip: expedite = 加快進程；同字根，都有「向前推進」的概念

lookalikes.zh: 迅速的 vs 吉祥的、吉兆的
lookalikes.tip: auspicious = 吉利的，字尾相同 -icious 但意思完全不同

meanings.en: 禮貌的行為或態度
meanings.zh: the quality of being respectful and considerate in behavior
examples: In Japanese culture, politeness is deeply embedded in communication style.
Sometimes excessive politeness in meetings prevents honest feedback.

meanings.en: （語言學）禮貌策略
meanings.zh: in linguistics, strategies used to maintain social harmony
examples: Using indirect language is a form of politeness in many cultures.

collocations.zh: 直接反饋 vs 表面客氣

similar.diff: courtesy 更正式、儀式感更強
manners 指具體行為；politeness 是整體特質
tact 強調說話得體、不傷人，比 politeness 更強調技巧

lookalikes.zh: 禮貌 vs 政治
lookalikes.tip: polite 和 politics 都源自希臘文 polis（城邦），但意思早已分岔

lookalikes.zh: 有禮貌的 vs 不禮貌的
lookalikes.tip: im- 字首表示否定，是常見的反義構詞方式

meanings.en: （疾病的）症狀、徵兆
meanings.zh: a sign of illness felt by the patient
examples: Common symptoms of burnout include fatigue and reduced performance.

meanings.en: （比喻）某問題的徵兆
meanings.zh: a sign that a larger problem exists
examples: High churn is a symptom of a deeper product-market fit issue.
Poor communication is a symptom, not the root cause.

collocations.en: a symptom of a larger problem

similar.diff: 醫學上 sign 是醫生觀察的客觀現象；symptom 是病人感受到的
更中性、廣泛；symptom 通常暗示有問題存在
warning sign 更強調預警性質

lookalikes.zh: 症狀 vs 症候群
lookalikes.tip: syndrome = 一組 symptoms 同時出現的病症，如 burnout syndrome

lookalikes.zh: 有症狀的 vs 無症狀的
lookalikes.tip: a- 字首表示「沒有、相反」，COVID 期間這個詞大量出現在新聞中

meanings.en: （學校或課程的）課程綱要、全部課程內容
meanings.zh: the subjects and content included in a course of study
examples: The curriculum was redesigned to include more hands-on projects.
Our onboarding curriculum covers product vision, roadmap, and team norms.

meanings.en: （比喻）一套有系統的學習內容
meanings.zh: a structured set of things to learn
examples: She built a curriculum for new PMs joining the team.

similar.diff: syllabus 是單一課程的大綱；curriculum 是整個學程的全部課程
更廣泛，可指任何有系統的計畫
course 是 curriculum 的組成單位

lookalikes.zh: 單數 vs 複數（拉丁文複數）
lookalikes.tip: curriculum 是拉丁文借詞，正式複數是 curricula，不是 curriculums

lookalikes.zh: 課程 vs 履歷（CV）
lookalikes.tip: curriculum vitae 字面意思是「人生的跑道」，就是你的履歷

meanings.en: 已具備所需工具、技能或資源的；有準備的
meanings.zh: having the necessary tools, skills, or resources
examples: Are you equipped to handle this level of ambiguity as a PM?
The team is well-equipped to tackle the technical challenges ahead.

meanings.en: equip 的過去式——為某人提供所需的工具或能力
meanings.zh: past tense of equip: to provide with necessary tools or skills
examples: The training equipped her with the skills to lead cross-functional teams.

similar.diff: prepared 強調心理準備；equipped 強調工具或能力的配備
armed with 強調帶著某種優勢出發
capable 強調本身能力；equipped 強調外部工具和資源到位

lookalikes.zh: 過去式 / 形容詞 vs 原形動詞
lookalikes.tip: equip → equipped，雙 p 是常見拼字陷阱

lookalikes.zh: 設備齊全 vs 準備充分
lookalikes.tip: well-equipped 強調有工具資源；well-prepared 強調有計劃心理準備，語意接近但出發點不同

meanings.en: 物理上的摩擦力
meanings.zh: the resistance between two surfaces in contact
examples: Friction between the parts causes heat buildup.

meanings.en: （比喻）阻力、衝突、不順暢
meanings.zh: conflict or resistance between people or in a process
examples: There's too much friction in the checkout flow — users are dropping off.
Reduce friction between teams by aligning on goals.

collocations.zh: 無阻力的體驗（UX 術語）

similar.diff: resistance 可指物理或心理阻力；friction 更強調接觸面的衝突
tension 常指人際或情境的緊繃
obstacle 是具體的障礙物；friction 是抽象的阻力感

lookalikes.zh: 摩擦阻力 vs 牽引力、進展
lookalikes.tip: traction = 獲得動力和進展；friction = 阻礙進展，意思相反但字尾相同

lookalikes.zh: 無摩擦的 vs 無縫的
lookalikes.tip: frictionless 和 seamless 在 UX 語境中幾乎同義，都形容體驗流暢

meanings.en: 划船（row 的動名詞）
meanings.zh: the activity of propelling a boat with oars
examples: She took up rowing as a form of cross-training.
They went rowing on the lake every Sunday.

meanings.en: （英式口語）爭吵、爭論
meanings.zh: (British informal) a noisy argument
examples: There was a huge rowing between stakeholders over the roadmap direction.

similar.diff: rowing 用雙槳固定在船上；paddling 用手持單槳
sculling 是競技用法，每隻手各持一支槳
canoeing 是坐在獨木舟裡划行

lookalikes.zh: 划船 vs 漫遊
lookalikes.tip: roam = 漫遊、流浪；rowing 和 roaming 發音和長相都有點像但意思完全不同

lookalikes.zh: 划船 vs 割草
lookalikes.tip: mow = 割草；mowing 是割草的動名詞，字尾相同 -owing

meanings.en: 颶風（大西洋地區的熱帶氣旋）
meanings.zh: a tropical storm with very strong winds, occurring in the Atlantic
examples: The project hit us like a hurricane — everything changed overnight.
Hurricane season runs from June to November in the Atlantic.

meanings.en: （比喻）來勢洶洶的事物
meanings.zh: something that arrives with great force and disruption
examples: She entered the meeting like a hurricane, reshaping the entire agenda.

family.zh: 颶風（無變形，但有衍生詞）

collocations.zh: 度過颶風（比喻：撐過危機）
颶風眼（比喻：危機中心的平靜）

similar.diff: 同類風暴，颱風在西太平洋，颶風在大西洋
印度洋和南半球的說法
更廣泛，hurricane 是 storm 的一種極端形式

lookalikes.zh: 颶風 vs 颱風
lookalikes.tip: 都是熱帶氣旋，只是地區不同叫法：大西洋叫 hurricane，西太平洋叫 typhoon

lookalikes.zh: 颶風 vs 颶風燈
lookalikes.tip: hurricane lamp = 防風燈，設計上能在颶風中不被吹熄

meanings.en: 在⋯⋯之外、超越（空間、時間、範圍）
meanings.zh: further than; more than
examples: Our goals go beyond just hitting KPIs.
The impact extends beyond what we initially expected.

meanings.en: move beyond = 超越過去、向前走
meanings.zh: to progress past something; to no longer be limited by something
examples: It's time to move beyond our reliance on manual processes.
We need to move beyond the feature-factory mindset.

family.zh: 超越（可作名詞：the great beyond = 來世）

collocations.zh: 排除合理懷疑（法律用語）

similar.diff: 更正式、帶有哲學意涵
強調在數量或品質上超越
exceed 偏數字或範圍上的超過；beyond 更抽象

lookalikes.zh: 超越 vs 在⋯⋯之下
lookalikes.tip: beneath = 在下方；beyond = 在外面、超越，方向完全不同

lookalikes.zh: 超越 vs 在⋯⋯旁邊
lookalikes.tip: beside = 在旁邊；besides = 除此之外，這三個字都長很像

meanings.en: 大拇指
meanings.zh: the short thick finger on the hand
examples: He gave a thumbs up after the demo.
She hurt her thumb while moving furniture.

meanings.en: 用拇指翻閱；搭便車
meanings.zh: to flip through pages; or to hitchhike
examples: She thumbed through the research report.
He thumbed a ride to the conference.

family.zh: 拇指螺絲（歷史酷刑工具）

collocations.zh: 讚 / 踩（同意 / 不同意）

similar.diff: thumb 專指大拇指；finger 是其他四指
scroll 是數位時代的 thumb through
browse 更廣泛，可指網路或書架瀏覽

lookalikes.zh: 大拇指 vs 重擊、砰地一聲
lookalikes.tip: thump = 用力打或砰的聲音；多一個 p，意思差很多

lookalikes.zh: 縮圖 vs 概略描述
lookalikes.tip: thumbnail sketch 字面是「指甲大小的素描」，比喻對某事的簡短描述

meanings.en: 被放大的、增強的（amplify 的過去式）
meanings.zh: made larger, louder, or stronger
examples: The problem was amplified by poor communication across teams.
Social media amplified the product feedback loop.

meanings.en: 詳述、進一步說明（正式用法）
meanings.zh: to expand on or add detail to something said
examples: Could you amplify on what you mean by 'product-led growth'?

similar.diff: magnify 更偏向視覺放大；amplify 偏向聲音或影響力
intensify 強調強度增加；amplify 強調規模或聲量擴大
boost 更口語，強調快速提升

lookalikes.zh: 過去式 / 形容詞 vs 放大器（名詞）
lookalikes.tip: amplifier 是做放大這件事的裝置或人

lookalikes.zh: 振幅 vs 態度
lookalikes.tip: amplitude 是物理學術語指波的幅度；attitude = 態度，字尾相似

meanings.en: （海浪、水面）波濤起伏的
meanings.zh: having many small waves; rough
examples: The waters were too choppy for kayaking.

meanings.en: （比喻）不流暢的、斷斷續續的
meanings.zh: lacking smooth flow; jerky or disjointed
examples: The presentation felt choppy — the transitions between slides were abrupt.
The user journey is too choppy; we need smoother onboarding steps.

family.zh: 波濤起伏的狀態；不流暢感

collocations.zh: 波濤洶湧的水面（也比喻困難時期）

similar.diff: rough 更廣泛；choppy 強調短促起伏的感覺
turbulent 更嚴重，choppy 是小幅波動
jagged 強調形狀上的不規則；choppy 強調節奏或流暢度

lookalikes.zh: 形容詞 vs 動詞：砍切
lookalikes.tip: chop = 砍、切；choppy = 被砍切後的效果（波浪或節奏感）

lookalikes.zh: 不流暢的 vs 感情用事的
lookalikes.tip: soppy = 過於感情用事的（英式口語）；字尾 -oppy 相同但意思完全不同

meanings.en: 浮潛用的呼吸管
meanings.zh: a breathing tube used for swimming just below the surface
examples: She packed her snorkel and fins for the Hawaii trip.

meanings.en: 進行浮潛
meanings.zh: to swim using a snorkel
examples: We went snorkeling in the Great Barrier Reef.

meanings.en: （軍事）潛水艇通氣管
meanings.zh: a device allowing a submarine to run its engine underwater
examples: The submarine used its snorkel to recharge batteries without surfacing.

similar.diff: snorkel 在水面附近；scuba 可潛入深水
swim 是泛指；snorkel 特指用呼吸管游
freedive 靠憋氣，不使用任何輔助裝置

lookalikes.zh: 浮潛 vs 打鼾
lookalikes.tip: snore = 打鼾；snorkel 的水中呼吸聲讓人聯想到打鼾聲

lookalikes.zh: 呼吸管 vs 動物的鼻口部
lookalikes.tip: snout = 動物的鼻子和嘴部；snorkel 的外形有點像動物的 snout

meanings.en: 水肺潛水裝備（縮寫：Self-Contained Underwater Breathing Apparatus）
meanings.zh: self-contained underwater breathing apparatus
examples: He got his scuba certification last summer.

meanings.en: 進行水肺潛水
meanings.zh: to dive using scuba equipment
examples: We went scuba diving in the Maldives.

meanings.en: 水肺潛水這項活動
meanings.zh: the activity of diving with scuba equipment
examples: Scuba allows divers to stay underwater for extended periods.

similar.diff: snorkeling 在水面；scuba 可潛入更深
freediving 靠憋氣；scuba 靠氧氣瓶
deep-sea diving 特指極深的潛水，scuba 是廣義的器材式潛水

lookalikes.zh: 水肺潛水 vs 森巴舞
lookalikes.tip: 只差中間一個字母，不要搞混了！

lookalikes.zh: 水肺潛水 vs 刷洗；取消
lookalikes.tip: scrub = 用力刷洗，也可指取消計畫；字母順序稍有不同

meanings.en: 犯罪者、罪魁禍首；（比喻）問題的主因
meanings.zh: a person or thing responsible for a problem or offense
examples: The real culprit behind the bug was an unhandled edge case.
Slow page load was identified as the culprit for high bounce rates.

meanings.en: （法律古語）待受審的被告
meanings.zh: (archaic legal) a person accused of a crime
examples: The culprit was brought before the court.

similar.diff: perpetrator 更正式法律用語；culprit 更口語且可用於比喻
cause 是中性詞；culprit 帶有「應受責怪」的意涵
offender 強調違反規則；culprit 更強調「是誰的錯」

lookalikes.zh: 罪犯 vs 文化
lookalikes.tip: 開頭 cul- 相同，但完全不同的詞

lookalikes.zh: 有過失的 vs 有能力的
lookalikes.tip: culpable = 應受責備；capable = 有能力；字形相似但意思相反

meanings.en: 授權、使命；（政府或機構的）任務授權
meanings.zh: an official order or authorization to do something
examples: Our team's mandate is to improve retention by 20%.
The new CEO has a clear mandate to transform the culture.

meanings.en: 強制規定、授權要求
meanings.zh: to officially require or authorize something
examples: The board mandated a full product audit.
The policy mandates quarterly reviews.

family.zh: 被授權的；規定了（過去式）

similar.diff: directive 是從上而下的命令；mandate 帶有授權和責任的意涵
authority 是抽象的；mandate 是具體的任務委託
commission 更強調委託某人做某件具體的事

lookalikes.zh: 名詞：授權 vs 形容詞：強制的
lookalikes.tip: mandatory = 強制的（形容詞）；mandate = 授權 / 強制規定（名詞 / 動詞）

lookalikes.zh: 授權 vs 責任；地函
lookalikes.tip: mantle = 一件外套，也比喻責任或角色（take on the mantle of）；字形和 mandate 有點像

meanings.en: 動物的角
meanings.zh: a hard pointed growth on the head of an animal
examples: The bull lowered its horn and charged.
Rhinoceros horns are made of keratin, not bone.

meanings.en: （車子的）喇叭；（音樂的）號角、管樂器
meanings.zh: a warning device on a vehicle; or a brass musical instrument
examples: He honked the horn in traffic.
She plays the French horn in the orchestra.

collocations.en: on the horns of a dilemma

similar.diff: alarm 更廣泛；horn 特指發出號聲的裝置
siren 用於緊急車輛；horn 是一般喇叭
antler 專指鹿的分叉角；horn 是實心的角（牛、犀牛等）

lookalikes.zh: 角 / 喇叭 vs 玉米
lookalikes.tip: 只差一個字母，發音也很像，注意區分！

lookalikes.zh: 兩種寫法都有
lookalikes.tip: foghorn 是大聲的警報，也比喻說話聲音非常大的人

meanings.en: （鳥或飛機）俯衝、急降
meanings.zh: to move rapidly downward through the air
examples: The eagle swooped down to catch its prey.
The helicopter swooped in for a rescue.

meanings.en: 突然出現並奪取或介入
meanings.zh: to arrive suddenly and take or do something
examples: A competitor swooped in and acquired the startup we were eyeing.

meanings.en: in one fell swoop = 一舉、一次搞定
meanings.zh: all at once; with a single decisive action
examples: We fixed three bugs in one fell swoop.

similar.diff: dive 更廣泛；swoop 強調從上方急速俯衝
descend 更緩慢、正式
plunge 更強調力道和速度，常帶有衝入的意涵

lookalikes.zh: 俯衝 vs 掃過、橫掃
lookalikes.tip: sweep = 橫向掃過；swoop = 由上往下急速衝擊

lookalikes.zh: 俯衝 vs 偷偷打探
lookalikes.tip: snoop = 偷窺、探聽別人的事；字形有點像但意思不同

meanings.en: 介入、干預（某情況或衝突）
meanings.zh: to come between parties or events to change what is happening
examples: The manager had to intervene when the two teams couldn't agree.
Regulators may intervene if the company continues these practices.

meanings.en: （時間或事件）在中間發生
meanings.zh: to occur between events in time
examples: Several months intervened between the proposal and the final decision.

family.zh: 干預主義的 / 干預者
居中的、介入的（如 intervening period 中間這段時間）

similar.diff: intercede 強調為他人說情；intervene 更中性
mediate 是以中立者身份調解
interfere 帶有更強的負面意涵，暗示不受歡迎的介入

lookalikes.zh: 介入 vs 面試、採訪
lookalikes.tip: inter- 都是「在⋯⋯之間」，-vene 是「來」；-view 是「看」——面試是雙方互看！

lookalikes.zh: 介入 vs 阻止
lookalikes.tip: intervene = 介入改變情況；prevent = 阻止某事發生，目的不同

meanings.en: 跳躍；突然快速移動
meanings.zh: to jump far or high; to move suddenly and quickly
examples: The startup took a leap of faith and pivoted.
She leaped at the opportunity to lead the new product.

meanings.en: 跳躍；重大進步
meanings.zh: a big jump; a major advance
examples: This is a leap forward in our data infrastructure.
It was a huge leap from intern to senior PM in three years.

family.zh: 跨越超越（比喻）；青蛙跳遊戲
leap 的過去式（英式 leapt；美式 leaped）

similar.diff: jump 更口語；leap 更有力、更強調距離或幅度
bound 強調連續跳躍
spring 強調彈力帶動的跳躍

lookalikes.zh: 跳躍 vs 一堆
lookalikes.tip: heap = 一堆、一大量；leap = 跳躍，只差第一個字母

lookalikes.zh: 跳蛙遊戲 vs 跨越競爭對手的策略
lookalikes.tip: leapfrog strategy = 直接跳過現有競爭格局，採用更先進技術的商業策略

meanings.en: 囤積、大量積存（通常出於恐懼或貪婪）
meanings.zh: to collect and store large amounts of something, often secretly
examples: During the pandemic, people hoarded toilet paper.
The product team was hoarding data instead of sharing insights.

meanings.en: 囤積的物品、大量儲存
meanings.zh: a large stock of something stored away
examples: They discovered a hoard of vintage game cartridges.
A hoard of gold coins was found buried in the garden.

family.zh: 囤積（動名詞）；廣告看板（英式）

similar.diff: stockpile 更中性，常用於戰略儲備；hoard 暗示過度、隱密
accumulate 更正式中性
amass 強調規模大，帶有刻意積累的意涵

lookalikes.zh: 囤積 vs 一大群人
lookalikes.tip: horde = 一大群人（尤指入侵的）；hoard = 囤積物，拼法差一個字母

lookalikes.zh: 囤積 vs 登機、登船；木板圍欄
lookalikes.tip: boarding = 登上交通工具；也可指臨時木板牆。hoarding 在英式英語中也可指廣告看板

meanings.en: 明確地、清楚地表達（不留含糊空間）
meanings.zh: in a clear and detailed manner, leaving nothing implied
examples: The requirements weren't explicitly stated, leading to misalignment.
We need to explicitly define what success looks like.

meanings.en: 直接地說出（毫不迴避）
meanings.zh: directly and without any vagueness or hesitation
examples: She explicitly told the team that the deadline was non-negotiable.

similar.diff: clearly 更口語廣泛；explicitly 強調沒有留下任何模糊空間
directly 強調方式直接；explicitly 強調內容完整無歧義
更正式；explicitly 更常用於日常和職場

lookalikes.zh: 明確地 vs 隱含地
lookalikes.tip: ex- = 向外（說出來）；im- = 向內（藏在裡面）；是一對反義詞

lookalikes.zh: 明確的 vs 成人內容
lookalikes.tip: explicit content 特指成人或限制級內容；要注意語境，避免誤用

title: lingering
hint: 揮之不去的、遲遲不散的

meanings.en: 持續存在的、揮之不去的（通常是令人不快的事物）
meanings.zh: lasting for a long time; slow to end or leave
examples: There's a lingering distrust between the two teams after the failed project.
The lingering question is whether users will actually pay for this.

meanings.en: （linger 的動名詞）逗留、徘徊
meanings.zh: staying or taking a long time to leave
examples: She was lingering at the whiteboard, reluctant to erase her ideas.

similar.diff: persistent 更中性；lingering 帶有「慢慢消失但還未消失」的感覺
enduring 更正面（持久的價值）；lingering 偏負面（揮之不去的問題）
residual 更中性偏技術；lingering 帶有情感的重量

lookalikes.zh: 揮之不去 vs 渴望、思念
lookalikes.tip: longing = 強烈渴望或思念某事物；lingering = 遲遲不散

lookalikes.zh: 逗留 vs 猛衝、刺擊
lookalikes.tip: lunge = 突然向前猛衝；和 linger 的起步字母相近但意思截然不同

meanings.en: 槓桿作用；影響力、優勢
meanings.zh: the power to influence a situation; mechanical advantage
examples: Data is our biggest leverage in this negotiation.
Having a large user base gives us significant leverage.

meanings.en: 善用、發揮（資源或優勢）
meanings.zh: to use something to maximum advantage
examples: We should leverage our existing user base to drive referrals.
Leverage your network to find the right candidates.

family.zh: 使用槓桿的（如 leveraged buyout 槓桿收購）

similar.diff: utilize 更正式、中性；leverage 帶有「最大化效益」的積極意涵
exploit 可以是負面的剝削；leverage 通常正面
capitalize on = 趁機利用某個機會，與 leverage 語意最接近

lookalikes.zh: 槓桿 vs 巨型事物（比喻）
lookalikes.tip: leviathan = 龐大的東西或組織；都以 lev- 開頭，但完全不同

lookalikes.zh: 槓桿（名詞）vs 槓桿力 / 善用（名詞 / 動詞）
lookalikes.tip: lever 是物理的槓桿；leverage 是它的衍生詞，指槓桿帶來的力量或比喻用法

meanings.en: 磨利（刀具）；精進、磨練（技能）
meanings.zh: to sharpen a blade; to refine and perfect a skill
examples: She spent years honing her craft as a product designer.
We need to hone our pitch before the investor meeting.

meanings.en: 磨刀石
meanings.zh: a whetstone used to sharpen blades
examples: He ran the knife along the hone before cooking.

collocations.zh: 聚焦於、鎖定（常見誤用，正確是 home in on）

similar.diff: 更口語，可指磨刀或提升能力
強調去除缺陷、讓事物更完善
強調讓某事物更光滑、更完美，常用於演講或文章

lookalikes.zh: 磨練 vs 鎖定目標
lookalikes.tip: 'hone in on' 是常見誤用，正確是 'home in on'（像鴿子歸巢般鎖定目標）

lookalikes.zh: 磨練 vs 語氣／肌肉緊實
lookalikes.tip: tone 可以是名詞（語氣）或動詞（緊實肌肉），長相相近

meanings.en: 依賴、依靠；信任
meanings.zh: the state of depending on or trusting something/someone
examples: Our team's reliance on manual processes is slowing us down.
Self-reliance is a key trait for senior PMs.

meanings.en: 對某事物的信任程度
meanings.zh: the degree to which something is trusted or depended upon
examples: Our reliance on third-party APIs creates a single point of failure.

similar.diff: 語氣更強，常帶負面含意（如藥物依賴）
reliance 強調行為上的依靠；trust 更偏情感信任
confidence 是對自己或某事的把握，reliance 是對外部事物的依靠

lookalikes.zh: 依賴 vs 聯盟
lookalikes.tip: alliance 是 al- + liance，指兩方合作；reliance 是 re- + liance，指依靠

lookalikes.zh: 可靠的 vs 依賴的
lookalikes.tip: reliable = 值得信賴的（描述被依靠的事物）；reliant = 依賴某物的（描述依賴者）

meanings.en: 保證、擔保；讓人放心的話
meanings.zh: a confident statement that something is true or will happen
examples: The PM gave the team assurance that the deadline would not change.
Despite his assurances, the project was delayed.

meanings.en: 自信、篤定的態度
meanings.zh: confidence and certainty in one's manner
examples: She spoke with great assurance during the board presentation.

meanings.en: （英式英語）人壽保險
meanings.zh: life assurance (UK term for life insurance)
examples: He took out a life assurance policy.

collocations.zh: 品質保證（職場常見縮寫）

similar.diff: guarantee 更正式、具法律約束力；assurance 偏向口頭或情感上的保證
promise 是個人承諾；assurance 更強調消除對方的疑慮
confidence 是內在的信心；assurance 是給別人的保證

lookalikes.zh: 保證 vs 保險
lookalikes.tip: insurance 是商業保險產品；assurance 在英式英語中特指人壽保險，在美式英語中多指保證

lookalikes.zh: 使放心 vs 確保
lookalikes.tip: assure = 讓某人放心；ensure = 確保某事發生（採取行動）

meanings.en: repetitions 的縮寫，指（運動的）次數、組數
meanings.zh: short for repetitions — the number of times you do an exercise
examples: Do 3 sets of 10 reps.
More reps with lighter weights builds endurance.

meanings.en: representatives 的縮寫，指業務代表、銷售代表
meanings.zh: short for representatives — sales or customer service reps
examples: Our sales reps are hitting their quarterly targets.
Customer service reps need strong empathy skills.

family.zh: 名聲（rep 也可以是這個的縮寫）

similar.diff: 正式說法，口語中幾乎都說 reps
reps 是每組的次數；sets 是總共幾組
正式說法，縮寫 reps 在商業場合很常見

lookalikes.zh: 重複次數／代表 vs 說唱 / 敲打
lookalikes.tip: rap = 說唱（音樂）或輕敲，發音相同只是拼法不同

lookalikes.zh: 代表 vs 名聲
lookalikes.tip: rep 也可以是 reputation 的縮寫：'She has a great rep in the industry.'

meanings.en: 自滿的、得過且過的；因滿足現狀而失去警惕
meanings.zh: smug and uncritically satisfied with oneself or one's situation; unaware of potential risks
examples: After a successful launch, the team became complacent and stopped iterating.
We can't afford to be complacent — competitors are catching up.

meanings.en: 對問題漠然、不採取行動的
meanings.zh: showing a lack of concern about problems that need attention
examples: The complacent attitude of management led to the company falling behind.

similar.diff: 語氣稍輕，complacent 更強調忽視風險的危險性
smug 帶有更強的負面含意，暗示讓人反感的自大
content 是中性甚至正面的；complacent 帶有負面的「鬆懈、危險」意味

lookalikes.zh: 自滿的 vs 順從的、討好的
lookalikes.tip: complaisant（/kəmˈpleɪ.zənt/）= 願意取悅他人的，差一個字母但意思完全不同！

lookalikes.zh: 自滿 vs 遵從、合規
lookalikes.tip: compliance 是遵守規則；complacency 是自滿放鬆，是 PM 的大忌

meanings.en: 少得可憐的、貧乏的；（數量或品質）不足的
meanings.zh: lacking in quantity or quality; very small or insufficient
examples: The startup launched with a meager budget of $5,000.
The report offered only meager evidence to support the claim.

meanings.en: （人）瘦弱的、骨瘦如柴的（較古典用法）
meanings.zh: (of a person) thin and weak
examples: The prisoner looked meager and exhausted after months of hardship.

similar.diff: 強調供給不足；meager 強調數量少到不夠用
更正式、客觀的說法
sparse 常形容分散、稀疏；meager 強調量的貧乏

lookalikes.zh: 貧乏的 vs 熱切的
lookalikes.tip: 都是 -ager 結尾，但意思完全不同！eager = 非常熱衷的

lookalikes.zh: 美式拼法 vs 英式拼法
lookalikes.tip: 同一個字，美式用 meager，英式用 meagre

title: outside-in
hint: 由外而內（從顧客角度出發）

meanings.en: 由外而內的（思維方式）；從顧客或市場角度出發，再回頭設計內部流程或產品
meanings.zh: starting from the customer or market perspective rather than internal processes
examples: We need to adopt an outside-in approach and start with what users actually need.
An outside-in strategy means listening to customers before building features.

meanings.en: （描述方向）由外往內
meanings.zh: from the outside to the inside
examples: The wind turned the umbrella outside-in.

family.zh: 由外而內（兼作形容詞和副詞）

similar.diff: 強調以客戶為核心，outside-in 強調思考的出發點和方向
更偏向設計領域的用語
強調市場力量（競爭、需求）主導決策

lookalikes.zh: 由外而內 vs 由內而外
lookalikes.tip: inside-out = 從公司內部能力或技術出發來決定做什麼，兩者是相反的策略思維

lookalikes.zh: 外部視角 vs 由上而下／由下而上
lookalikes.tip: top-down 和 bottom-up 是指組織內部的方向，outside-in 是外部到內部的視角

meanings.en: 想像、設想（未來的狀態或可能性）
meanings.zh: to imagine or picture something in the future
examples: I envision a product that makes onboarding effortless.
Can you envision what the user experience will look like in three years?

meanings.en: 預期某事發生（帶有計劃性的想像）
meanings.zh: to expect or plan for something to happen
examples: We envision launching in three new markets by Q4.

family.zh: 設想（英式，與 envision 幾乎同義）

similar.diff: 更口語、廣泛；envision 更強調帶有目的性的未來規劃
更強調在腦中形成清晰的圖像
foresee 強調預測某事會發生；envision 更強調主動描繪一個理想狀態

lookalikes.zh: 兩者幾乎同義
lookalikes.tip: envisage 更常見於英式英語；envision 更常見於美式英語，PM 簡報中 envision 更常用

lookalikes.zh: 動詞：設想 vs 名詞：願景
lookalikes.tip: vision 是名詞（產品願景）；envision 是動詞（去設想）

meanings.en: 動作、移動；（會議中的）提案、動議
meanings.zh: movement; or a formal proposal in a meeting
examples: The team seconded the motion to postpone the release.
The app uses motion design to guide users through the flow.

meanings.en: 用手勢示意某人做某事
meanings.zh: to signal or gesture to someone
examples: She motioned for everyone to sit down.

meanings.en: set something in motion = 啟動某件事、讓某事開始運轉
meanings.zh: to start a process
examples: The CEO's announcement set a major reorganization in motion.

similar.diff: movement 更強調位移或社會運動；motion 更常用於物理學或正式程序
在會議語境中 motion 是正式用法；proposal 更廣泛
gesture 是肢體語言；motion（動詞）也有用手示意的意思

lookalikes.zh: 動作／動議 vs 情緒
lookalikes.tip: e-motion 字面是「往外動的東西」，情緒讓你有所行動——有趣的字源！

lookalikes.zh: 移動 vs 想法、概念
lookalikes.tip: notion = 一個模糊的想法或概念；motion = 移動或動議，只差一個字母

meanings.en: 現象級的、非凡的、驚人的（口語中表示極好）
meanings.zh: remarkably exceptional; extraordinary
examples: The product achieved phenomenal growth in its first quarter.
She did a phenomenal job leading the launch.

meanings.en: （學術用法）現象的、感官可感知的
meanings.zh: relating to phenomena; perceptible by the senses
examples: Kant distinguished between the phenomenal world and the noumenal world.

similar.diff: 更正式；phenomenal 口語中更強調讓人驚嘆的程度
強調優於一般水平；phenomenal 語氣更強烈
remarkable 較為中性；phenomenal 帶有強烈讚嘆

lookalikes.zh: 形容詞：非凡的 vs 名詞：現象
lookalikes.tip: phenomenon（單數）→ phenomena（複數），注意複數不加 s！

lookalikes.zh: 非凡的 vs 現象學的
lookalikes.tip: phenomenological 是哲學術語，跟日常用法的 phenomenal 完全不同

title: perpetuate
hint: 使永久持續（常指負面事物）

meanings.en: 使⋯⋯永久持續、使⋯⋯長期存在（常用於負面事物）
meanings.zh: to make something continue indefinitely, especially something negative
examples: Poor design decisions can perpetuate bad user habits.
The policy unintentionally perpetuated inequality.

meanings.en: 使（記憶、傳統）永遠留存
meanings.zh: to preserve something so it continues to be remembered
examples: The monument was built to perpetuate the memory of the founder.

similar.diff: sustain 偏中性，可以是正面的；perpetuate 常帶有負面或「本不應如此」的意涵
prolong 強調時間上的拉長；perpetuate 強調讓某事「永遠存在」
reinforce 強調讓某事變得更強；perpetuate 強調讓它持續下去

lookalikes.zh: 使永久存在 vs 犯（罪）、實施（惡行）
lookalikes.tip: perpetrate = 犯下罪行（the perpetrator 是犯罪者）；兩個字差了中間幾個字母，意思天差地別！

lookalikes.zh: 形容詞：永久的 vs 動詞：使永久化
lookalikes.tip: perpetual motion machine（永動機）；perpetuate a system（使一個制度永久存在）

meanings.en: 脆弱性、易受傷害的狀態；（資安）漏洞
meanings.zh: the quality of being exposed to harm; a weakness that can be exploited
examples: Brené Brown argues that vulnerability is the birthplace of connection.
The team identified a critical security vulnerability in the authentication flow.

meanings.en: （比喻）暴露在風險中的狀態
meanings.zh: exposure to risk or potential harm
examples: Market vulnerability increases during economic downturns.

family.zh: 不易受傷的、堅不可摧的

similar.diff: weakness 更廣泛；vulnerability 強調「容易被攻擊或傷害」的狀態
fragility 強調容易破碎；vulnerability 強調容易被傷害或利用
exposure 強調暴露的狀態；vulnerability 強調本身的弱點

lookalikes.zh: 脆弱性 vs 耐久性
lookalikes.tip: durable = 耐用的；vulnerable = 脆弱的，是反義的概念，字尾都是 -ability

lookalikes.zh: 脆弱的 vs 話很多的、健談的
lookalikes.tip: voluble（/ˈvɒl.jʊ.bəl/）= 滔滔不絕的，拼法相近但意思完全不同！

title: assured
hint: 有把握的；確定的；被保證的

meanings.en: 有把握的、自信的
meanings.zh: confident and showing no doubts
examples: She spoke in an assured tone during the presentation.
He seemed completely assured of his decision.

meanings.en: 確定的、已成定局的
meanings.zh: certain to happen; guaranteed
examples: Victory seemed assured after the competitor pulled out.
With this deal, our funding is assured.

meanings.en: assure 的過去式／過去分詞：被保證、被告知可以放心
meanings.zh: past tense of assure — told something confidently to remove doubt
examples: I was assured that the data would be kept confidential.
You can rest assured — we'll handle it.

collocations.zh: 放心、可以確定（固定用法）

similar.diff: confident 是主動的自信；assured 帶有「已被確認、有依據」的安心感
certain 更廣泛；assured 強調來自外部保證或充分依據的確定
guaranteed 更正式，常有契約或承諾的意涵；assured 語感更個人

lookalikes.zh: 有把握的 vs 被確保（採取行動）
lookalikes.tip: assured 重點是人的心理狀態（放心、有把握）；ensured 重點是採取了行動讓某結果一定發生

lookalikes.zh: 自信有把握的 vs 有自信的
lookalikes.tip: 兩者幾乎同義，self-assured 帶有更沉穩、不需要外界認可的語感

meanings.en: 強健的、結實的；（系統、計畫）穩健可靠的
meanings.zh: strong and healthy; (of a system or plan) sturdy and reliable
examples: We need a robust data pipeline before we launch.
The new framework is more robust than the old one.

meanings.en: （討論、辯論）有力而直接的
meanings.zh: vigorous and forceful
examples: There was a robust debate in the product review meeting.

similar.diff: 強調從困境中恢復的能力；robust 偏重本身夠強
偏指物理上的堅固；robust 可用於抽象概念
更口語，語氣較輕；robust 更有正式感

lookalikes.zh: 強健的 vs 強健性
lookalikes.tip: robust 是形容詞；robustness 是名詞，常用於科技或分析報告

lookalikes.zh: 穩健的 vs 崎嶇的／耐用的
lookalikes.tip: rugged 常指地形崎嶇或產品粗獷耐用；robust 強調整體可靠

meanings.en: 道德的、符合倫理的
meanings.zh: relating to right and wrong behavior
examples: It's a moral responsibility to be transparent with users.
The team faced a moral dilemma about how to use the data.

meanings.en: （故事的）教訓、寓意
meanings.zh: the lesson or message of a story
examples: The moral of this case study is: don't skip user research.

collocations.zh: 道德指南針（比喻判斷是非的能力）

similar.diff: 偏向規範與制度面；moral 更偏個人良知
語氣更正式，強調美德品格
強調做事有準則，不因情況改變

lookalikes.zh: 道德 vs 士氣
lookalikes.tip: morale（士氣）結尾多一個 e，是完全不同的字，很容易混淆

lookalikes.zh: 道德的 vs 不道德的
lookalikes.tip: im- 前綴表示否定；amoral 則是「不在乎道德」，語感又不同

title: unceremoniously
hint: 突然而粗魯地、毫不客氣地

meanings.en: 毫不客氣地、突然且無禮地（不給面子地）
meanings.zh: in a sudden and rude way, without politeness or ceremony
examples: He was unceremoniously removed from the project midway through.
The proposal was unceremoniously rejected without any explanation.

family.zh: 不拘禮節的、突然無禮的

similar.diff: 強調速度突然，不一定含無禮意味
直接指無禮；unceremoniously 多了「不給面子」的語氣
偏指言語上簡短冷漠；unceremoniously 偏指行為上的突然

lookalikes.zh: 毫不客氣地 vs 隆重地
lookalikes.tip: un- 前綴反轉意思；ceremoniously 是正式隆重，unceremoniously 是反其道而行

lookalikes.zh: 不拘禮節 vs 不必要
lookalikes.tip: 兩個字都有 un- 開頭，但字根完全不同，別看到 un- 就混淆

meanings.en: 諷刺、反諷（說的和意思相反）；事與願違的巧合
meanings.zh: using words to mean the opposite; or a situation that is the opposite of what you'd expect
examples: The irony is that we built a tool to save time but it took twice as long.
She said 'great job' with heavy irony after the launch failed.

collocations.zh: 戲劇性反諷（觀眾知道但角色不知道）

similar.diff: sarcasm 語氣更尖刻、帶攻擊性；irony 可以是溫和的
satire 是以作品形式批評社會；irony 是一種語言或情境技巧
paradox 強調邏輯上的矛盾；irony 強調結果與期望相反

lookalikes.zh: 諷刺（名詞）vs 諷刺的（形容詞）
lookalikes.tip: 'It's ironic that...' 和 'The irony is...' 意思一樣，只是詞性不同

lookalikes.zh: 反諷 vs 挖苦
lookalikes.tip: irony 不一定有惡意；sarcasm 通常帶有傷人意圖

meanings.en: （事物的）面向、層面、角度
meanings.zh: a particular part or feature of something
examples: User trust is a critical aspect of product design.
We need to consider every aspect of the user journey.

meanings.en: （建築）朝向、外觀
meanings.zh: the direction a building faces; the appearance of something
examples: The apartment has a southern aspect with lots of natural light.

similar.diff: dimension 更強調深度或多維結構；aspect 更日常
facet 源自寶石的切面，比 aspect 更強調「其中一小面」
element 強調組成部分；aspect 強調觀察角度

lookalikes.zh: 面向 vs 尊重／方面
lookalikes.tip: 'in this respect' 和 'in this aspect' 意思接近，但 respect 在這用法更正式

lookalikes.zh: 面向 vs 前景
lookalikes.tip: prospect 指未來的可能性；aspect 是當下的面向，字尾相同容易混

meanings.en: 業餘愛好者（非以此為職業的人）
meanings.zh: a person who does something as a hobby, not as a profession
examples: He's an amateur photographer who shoots on weekends.
The competition is open to both professionals and amateurs.

meanings.en: 業餘的；（貶義）不夠專業的
meanings.zh: done by amateurs; sometimes implying low quality
examples: The presentation looked amateur — no data, no structure.
This is amateur work; we need to redo it before the client meeting.

similar.diff: hobbyist 純指興趣，不帶貶義；amateur 有時暗示不夠好
novice 強調缺乏經驗；amateur 強調非職業身分
layperson 指對某領域不熟悉的人；amateur 有時有一定程度的熱情投入

lookalikes.zh: 業餘者 vs 業餘水準的
lookalikes.tip: amateurish 加了 -ish 後綴，語氣帶貶義，比 amateur 更強調「不專業」

lookalikes.zh: 業餘者 vs 電樞（電機零件）
lookalikes.tip: armature 是電機學術語，拼法相近但毫不相關

meanings.en: 獲得（益處、快樂等）；衍生自、源自
meanings.zh: to get something from a source; to originate from
examples: We can derive valuable insights from user behavior data.
The word 'product' is derived from Latin.

family.zh: 衍生物；衍生的（或缺乏原創性的）

similar.diff: obtain 更直接，強調取得結果；derive 強調從某來源提取
extract 更強調「取出」的動作；derive 更抽象
stem from 強調根源；derive from 也可指主動獲取

lookalikes.zh: 衍生 vs 剝奪
lookalikes.tip: deprive = 奪走某人的東西；de- 有「去除」的意思，別和 derive 搞混

lookalikes.zh: 衍生 vs 抵達
lookalikes.tip: 發音相近，但意思完全不同；derive 強調來源，arrive 強調到達

meanings.en: 將兩個或多個不同的事物混為一談、錯誤地視為相同
meanings.zh: to combine two or more things incorrectly, treating them as the same
examples: Don't conflate user engagement with user satisfaction — they're different.
People often conflate being busy with being productive.

similar.diff: confuse 更口語，指分不清楚；conflate 更正式，強調錯誤地合併
equate 說兩者相等；conflate 是把兩個不同的東西錯誤地合在一起
mix up 更口語；conflate 有學術或討論語氣

lookalikes.zh: 混為一談 vs 膨脹
lookalikes.tip: inflate 指誇大或充氣；conflate 的 con- 有「合在一起」的意思

lookalikes.zh: 混淆合併 vs 混淆不清
lookalikes.tip: conflate 強調「誤以為是同一件事」；confuse 強調「分不清楚」，語意有細微差異

meanings.en: 深入探討、追問（drill down into）；反覆練習
meanings.zh: to investigate in depth; to practice repeatedly
examples: Let's drill down into the data to find the root cause.
The team drilled the presentation until it felt natural.

meanings.en: 演練、訓練；鑽孔機
meanings.zh: a practice exercise; a tool for making holes
examples: We ran a fire drill to test the emergency response.
Use a drill to make holes in the wall.

collocations.zh: （軍事）訓練士官；比喻嚴格訓練者

similar.diff: 更口語，語感相近；drill down 更常見於商業或數據分析情境
investigate 更正式；drill down 強調層層深入
rehearse 偏指表演或演講的排練；drill 偏指反覆練習直到熟練

lookalikes.zh: 單次演練 vs 多次演練
lookalikes.tip: 名詞可數，drills 為複數；動詞三單現在式也是 drills

lookalikes.zh: 深入探討 vs 放大細節
lookalikes.tip: 兩者語感相近，但 drill down 更常見於數據分析；zoom in 更視覺化

meanings.en: 高尚的、有品德的；貴族的
meanings.zh: having high moral qualities; belonging to the nobility
examples: It's a noble goal to make education accessible to everyone.
She comes from a noble family with centuries of history.

meanings.en: 貴族（人）
meanings.zh: a person of noble rank
examples: The nobles had significant influence over the king's decisions.

collocations.zh: 注意：諾貝爾獎是 Nobel，不是 noble

similar.diff: honorable 強調行為讓人尊重；noble 更強調品格與精神層次
virtuous 強調道德行為；noble 有時含貴族氣質
admirable 是他人視角的評價；noble 更主觀描述內在品質

lookalikes.zh: 高尚的 vs 諾貝爾
lookalikes.tip: Nobel Prize 是人名（Alfred Nobel）；常被誤拼成 noble prize

lookalikes.zh: high-minded vs 義大利語貴族
lookalikes.tip: nobile 是義大利語，長得像但是不同語言，英文不會用到

meanings.en: 深深崇敬、景仰（某人或某事）
meanings.zh: to feel deep respect or admiration for someone or something
examples: She is revered as a pioneer in the field of UX design.
The team reveres the founding values of the company.

family.zh: 牧師（宗教頭銜）；受尊敬的

similar.diff: admire 是日常欽佩；revere 帶有更強烈甚至神聖的崇敬感
respect 是一般尊重；revere 強度更強，近乎崇拜
venerate 與 revere 幾乎同義，但更常用於宗教或歷史人物

lookalikes.zh: 崇敬 vs 反轉
lookalikes.tip: reverse 多了一個 s，意思完全不同；revere 是情感，reverse 是動作

lookalikes.zh: 崇敬 vs 嚴重的／嚴厲的
lookalikes.tip: severe 和 revere 發音相近，但 se- 開頭，意思大相徑庭

meanings.en: 整齊地、有條理地
meanings.zh: in a tidy and orderly way
examples: The report was neatly organized into three sections.
She neatly folded the documents and put them in the folder.

meanings.en: 巧妙地、恰好地（解決問題或配合得剛剛好）
meanings.zh: cleverly and conveniently
examples: This solution neatly addresses both the usability and cost concerns.
The two ideas don't fit together as neatly as you might think.

similar.diff: tidily 強調物理上的整齊；neatly 也可用於抽象的「邏輯清晰」
cleanly 強調無雜質；neatly 強調有條理
cleverly 強調聰明；neatly 強調恰好、無縫地吻合

lookalikes.zh: 整齊地 vs 幾乎
lookalikes.tip: 只差一個字母位置，nearly = 幾乎，neatly = 整齊地，閱讀時要特別注意

lookalikes.zh: 整齊的 vs 下一個
lookalikes.tip: 視覺相似度低，但初學者常把 neat 讀快時和 next 搞混

meanings.en: 保護（某人或某物）免受傷害或不適
meanings.zh: protecting someone or something from harm or difficulty
examples: The senior PM was shielding the team from executive pressure.
He kept shielding her from the bad news instead of being transparent.

meanings.en: 防護、遮蔽（行為或材料）
meanings.zh: the act of protecting; protective material
examples: Shielding your team from all conflict can actually hinder their growth.

similar.diff: protecting 更通用；shielding 強調主動擋在前面，像盾牌一樣
sheltering 更偏提供避難所；shielding 強調阻擋外部威脅
guarding 強調警戒防守；shielding 強調阻隔傷害

lookalikes.zh: 保護 vs 揮舞（權力）
lookalikes.tip: wielding power = 行使權力；shielding 是保護，兩個字押韻但意思完全不同

lookalikes.zh: 保護 vs 讓步／產出
lookalikes.tip: yield 可以是讓步或產出；shield 是防護，字形容易看錯

meanings.en: 不可避免地、必然地
meanings.zh: in a way that cannot be avoided or prevented
examples: Inevitably, the project ran over budget.
If you don't address technical debt, it will inevitably slow you down.

similar.diff: 幾乎同義，但 inevitably 更常出現在書面或正式語境
necessarily 強調邏輯上的必要；inevitably 強調結果無法逃避
predictably 帶有「早就料到」的語氣；inevitably 更強調無法改變

lookalikes.zh: 必然地 vs 最終地
lookalikes.tip: eventually 只說「最後會發生」，不一定是必然；inevitably 強調「無可避免」

lookalikes.zh: 不可避免的 vs 可避免的
lookalikes.tip: evitable 幾乎不用；但理解 in- 前綴（否定）有助於記憶整個字族
